sources: PokerCoaching.com (Jonathan Little full PDF + page text)
Upswing Poker (Lucid Poker solver screenshots described in articles)
rangeBaseline.notes: Ranges updated from synthesized v2.0 to verified v3.0. UTG range confirmed ~17.6% (GTO Wizard NL500 6-max 100bb). BB vs BTN defense rate confirmed 51.7% (Upswing). 3-bet bluffs text-verified from Lucid Poker screenshots (Upswing). All 6 range files cross-referenced and source-cited.

preflopChart.notes: SB vs BB: raise-or-fold. Do NOT limp. Limping from SB is a major leak — it gives BB a free look and you play OOP postflop.

preflopChart.notes: SB opens very wide so BB can defend wide and 3-bet polarized. Bluffs differ from BTN because we are OOP vs SB (need more equity in bluffs).

avoidList.group: BB vs UTG/HJ — offsuit aces to FOLD (verified vs synthesized: confirmed FOLD)
avoidList.hands: A2o, A3o, A4o, A5o, A6o, A7o vs UTG open | A2o-A5o vs HJ open
avoidList.why: Verified: BB vs UTG call range starts at AJo (mix), A9o clear call. A2o-A8o vs UTG = FOLD. Ace blocker alone does not justify defend against tight range.

avoidList.group: BB vs any position — offsuit garbage (verified confirms: hard folds)
avoidList.why: No source found these in any defend range. These are pure folds in all scenarios.

avoidList.group: Weak offsuit kings — verified fold thresholds
avoidList.hands: K7o and below vs BTN (explicitly cited), K8o and below vs CO, K9o and below vs HJ/UTG
avoidList.why: Upswing text-verified K7o as a BB vs BTN FOLD. King-rag offsuit OOP = weak made hands dominated by better kings.
SB vs BB is raise-or-fold. Limping is -EV: gives BB a free look, you play OOP without pot initiative, range is face-up as weak.

avoidList.hands: Any folded hand from CO/BTN when folded to | Any folded hand from SB vs only BB
avoidList.why: Session 3 showed 36% steal rate vs 60-80% GTO. Passing on A4o (CO), K6o (SB), T9o (CO) is leaving free money. Default: RAISE if folded to in late position.

topHands.title: KK into AA preflop all-in (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: Pure cooler. Not a leak. KK vs AA is unavoidable at 100bb. Nothing to fix preflop.

topHands.title: ATo in a 3-bet pot (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: Calm line. Called c-bet, stayed under control, value-bet river once checked to. Good template for medium-strength showdown hands.

topHands.title: 88 river bet/fold (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: Good discipline. One-pair hand extracted value and did not pay off a river raise.

topHands.title: QTo blind defend (Session 1) — verified LEAK
topHands.takeaway: Verified: QTo is in BB vs BTN call range (borderline). The leak was the hopeful turn stab without equity, not the preflop defend.

topHands.title: KJs flat and flop peel drift (Session 1) — verified study spot
topHands.takeaway: Verified: KJs is in UTG open range. If this was a BTN open vs BB, calling KJs is correct. The error was passive postflop play without a value-or-bluff plan.

topHands.title: 94o defend versus button open (Session 1) — verified LEAK
topHands.takeaway: Verified LEAK. 94o is not in BB vs BTN call range by any source. Hard fold.

topHands.title: AJo disciplined fold on third diamond (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: Good read-based fold. AJo is in BB vs UTG call range so preflop was correct; fold on river scare card was good discipline.

topHands.title: AKcc bluff-catch threshold spot (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: AKo is a BB vs UTG 3-bet hand. If hero flatted from BB this was underplaying the hand. Study whether flat or 3-bet was correct preflop.

topHands.title: QTo on an ace-high paired texture (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: QTo borderline call range. Postflop: ace-high paired board hits UTG range hard. Check-fold is likely correct on this runout.

topHands.title: JJ on A-5-5-K-A (Session 1)
topHands.takeaway: JJ is UTG opening hand. As a 3-bet hand it should be rare vs UTG (verified: TT+ is the 3-bet range). Study: what did preflop action look like?

sessionAnalysis.gtoDeviationNotes: Score 62/100 (lower = more leaks). 3 confirmed preflop range errors (ATo UTG open, 94o x2 BB defend, A8o BB defend). 0.9% 3-bet rate (verified target ~8-12% for 6-max). Postflop c-bet texture selection needs work.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — ATo not in verified UTG open range (UTG opens AKo, AQo, KQo offsuit only). Fold preflop.
preflopLeaks.correction: UTG offsuit opens: AKo, AQo, KQo only. AJo is borderline mix. ATo = fold.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — 94o not in BB vs BTN call range by any source. Verified fold.
preflopLeaks.correction: BB vs BTN: fold all hands below ~76o, K7o, Q8o thresholds. 94o is never a call.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONTEXT-DEPENDENT LEAK — A8o is in BB vs BTN call range but FOLD vs UTG/HJ/CO. Check villain position.
preflopLeaks.correction: A8o: call vs BTN/SB opens only. Fold vs UTG, HJ, CO. Verified from bb-defend.json scenarios.

pfrPct.verdict: CLOSE — slight low end but acceptable

threeBetPct.verdict: MAJOR LEAK — 0.9% is far below verified target; missing squeeze spots and BB defense 3-bets

bbDefendPct.verdict: OK — within range, issue is WHICH hands were defended

cbetPct.verdict: OK frequency, WRONG textures — 0% success vs highcard/paired boards

key_leaks: UTG range: opened ATo (verified non-open) — cost estimated ~$10-15 in rake + weak postflop spot
BB defend trash: 94o x2 and A8o (vs non-BTN) — combined leak ~$18.5
3-bet rate 0.9% vs target 8-12%: missing ~7-10 3-bets per 100 hands; estimated EV loss 0.5-1.0 BB/100
C-bet 0% success on highcard/paired boards: opponents are calling stations; adjust sizing or skip bluffs
Fold-to-cbet 4.3%: stop floating without equity or a turn raise plan
corrections_vs_v2: UNCHANGED: UTG ATo is still a confirmed leak
UPDATED: A8o leak is position-dependent (call vs BTN/SB, fold vs UTG/HJ/CO) — not a blanket fold
UPDATED: BB defend 46.7% is acceptable frequency; prior analysis incorrectly flagged this; issue is range composition
CONFIRMED: 94o is always a fold — verified by all sources

sessionAnalysis.gtoDeviationNotes: Score 55/100 (worse than Session 1). 0% 3-bet rate confirmed major ongoing leak. 2 BB defend errors. Postflop -$120 loss on QJh trips spot needs review — preflop was correct.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CLEAN — QJs IS in verified UTG open range. Preflop action was correct.
preflopLeaks.correction: No correction needed preflop. The -$120 loss was a postflop decision error.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — A2o vs UTG = fold (verified). A2o vs BTN = marginal mix at best. Without knowing villain position, likely a fold.
preflopLeaks.correction: A2o: verified fold vs UTG, HJ, CO. Mix vs BTN (Upswing lists A2o as a 3-bet bluff from BB vs BTN, not a call). Never call with A2o.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — 83s not in any BB defend range in any source. Too weak.
preflopLeaks.correction: 83s: fold from BB vs all opens. Suited connectors need to be 54s+ minimum, and only against wider opens (BTN/SB).

postflop_study_spots.analysis: QJs UTG open, flopped trips on QTQ. Facing turn raise + river barrel. WITH TRIPS and a straight on the board (QTJ), villain needs a full house or better to beat you. Pot odds on river: if villain bets ~2/3 pot, need ~28% equity to call. Against a range that could have QQ, TT, T9s (broadway), TT (full house), KJ/J9 (boat blockers)... this is a solver-dependent spot. LIKELY a thin call that should be studied but probably correct given trips.
postflop_study_spots.correction: Do not auto-fold trips. Study the exact sizing and board to determine correct call threshold. Trips are not weak — the question is how often villain has quads/boat.

postflop_study_spots.analysis: K9s: hit second pair on K-Q-J-9-4. This is a very coordinated board. K9 has second pair + inside knowledge the board has K and 9. Villain can have ATo (straight), JJ, QQ, KQ, etc. Calling multiple streets with second pair on a broadway-heavy board is a clear leak.
postflop_study_spots.correction: K9s on J-K-Q-9-4: reduce to 1 street of call max. Check-fold turn or fold to significant river bet. Second pair on broadway-heavy boards has very little equity.

threeBetPct.verdict: CRITICAL LEAK — 2nd session in a row at 0%, pattern confirmed

bbDefendPct.verdict: UNDER-DEFENDING — only 9 opps but 33.3% is too tight; could be defending too many folds

cbetPct.verdict: Slightly high, 0% success is the real problem

foldToCbetPct.verdict: CRITICAL — never folding to opponent c-bets; too many hero calls

key_leaks: 0% 3-bet rate (pattern from S1): missing 8-12% of range on 3-bet opportunities; estimated 1-2 BB/100 loss
BB defend errors: A2o (fold vs UTG), 83s (fold always) — range pollution
0% fold-to-cbet across 10 opportunities: calling too wide without equity or a plan
QJh trips loss: likely correct call (study), not an unambiguous error
K9s second pair multi-street: definite leak — reduce to one call max on broadway-heavy boards
corrections_vs_v2: UPDATED: A2o is not just a 'fold vs tight opens' — it's NEVER a call from BB; it's a 3-bet bluff at very specific frequencies (BTN only) or fold
UPDATED: QJh UTG open confirmed clean — prior analysis was already correct
NEW: K9s second pair on JKQ94 identified as explicit postflop leak — broadway board awareness
CONFIRMED: 83s always fold — no source includes this in any defend range

sessionAnalysis.gtoDeviationNotes: Score 72/100 — best of 3 sessions. 3-bet rate improved to 5.1% (closer to target). PFR 12.1% is confirmed too low vs target 18-22%. 7 missed steal spots confirmed as leaks. BB defend tighten needed for A4o/85s vs UTG.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — A4o not in BB vs UTG call range (verified: A9o minimum, A2o-A8o = fold vs UTG). Fold.
preflopLeaks.correction: BB vs UTG: A9o+ call, AJo 3-bet or call. A4o is a clear fold.

preflopLeaks.verdict: CONFIRMED LEAK — 85s not in BB vs UTG call range (verified: 87s minimum for suited connectors vs UTG). Fold.
preflopLeaks.correction: BB vs UTG suited connectors: 87s, 76s, 65s, 64s, 54s. 85s and below = fold.

missed_steal_spots.verdict: MISSED STEAL — A4o is in CO open range (CO opens A5o+). Should be a raise.
MISSED STEAL — T9o is in CO open range (CO opens T9o-JTo). Should be a raise.
MISSED STEAL — K6o is in SB open range (SB opens K5o+). Should be a raise vs BB.
BORDERLINE — 83o is very bottom of SB range. Mix raise/fold, but leaning raise vs passive BB.
MISSED STEAL — T8s is in SB open range (SB opens T5s+). Should be a raise.
MISSED STEAL — 45o is in BTN range (BTN opens 54o+). Should be a raise vs BB-only.
BORDERLINE — 42o is barely in BTN range. Mix raise/fold depending on BB tendency.

pfrPct.verdict: CONFIRMED MAJOR LEAK — 6-10 percentage points below target. PFR vs VPIP gap of 15 pts means too many calls instead of raises.

threeBetPct.verdict: IMPROVEMENT from sessions 1-2 (0%), still below target. Continue adding 3-bets.

stealPct.verdict: CRITICAL LEAK — 36.4% vs 60-80% target. 7 confirmed missed steal spots. This is the #1 priority fix.

bbDefendPct.verdict: GOOD frequency — now tighten range composition (remove A4o, 85s vs UTG)

cbetSuccessPct.verdict: 0% success across all 3 sessions — opponents are sticky; adjust strategy

positive_plays: AQo BTN value bet on A-J-T-5-2 (+$40.49): BTN open, hit top pair, ran out value on clean board. Textbook.
AQs BB 3-bet pot vs A-7-Q (+$85.69): BB 3-bet with AQs (verified: 3-bet range), flopped top two, extracted max value.
3-bet with KK, KQs, AQs: verified correct 3-bet hands all executed. Improvement from prior sessions.
key_leaks: Steal rate 36.4% vs 60-80% target: single biggest EV leak this session, 7 confirmed missed spots
PFR 12.1% vs 18-22%: VPIP/PFR gap = excessive flatting; default to raise from late positions
BB defend errors: A4o and 85s vs UTG — tighten to verified chart (A9o+ offsuit, 87s+ suited connectors vs UTG)
C-bet 0% success rate persists: 3rd consecutive session; stop c-betting highcard boards without equity against calling stations
22.2% showdown win rate (4/18): too many weak hands reaching showdown; fold earlier or have cleaner range
corrections_vs_v2: CONFIRMED: A4o vs UTG is a fold — verified by bb-defend.json BB vs UTG scenario
CONFIRMED: 85s vs UTG is a fold — suited connector minimum vs UTG is 87s
NEW: 7 missed steal spots now individually identified and verified against range files
UPDATED: 3-bet rate 5.1% = improvement, not just a flat 'still too low' — acknowledge progress
NEW: C-bet 0% success across ALL 3 sessions is now clearly a pool adaptation problem — these players don't fold flops

crossSessionPatterns.action: Continue adding 3-bets. Specifically: BB vs BTN steal = 3-bet TT+, AQs+, plus A4s-A5s as bluffs. SB vs BTN = 3-bet TT+, AQs+, KQs.

crossSessionPatterns.pattern: C-bet success: 16.7% → 0% → 0% (across 3 sessions, 22 total c-bet attempts)
crossSessionPatterns.trend: Pool does not fold to c-bets. ADAPT.
crossSessionPatterns.action: Stop bluffing unimproved hands via c-bet. Check back more flops. When c-betting, use larger size (75%+) on dry boards to get folds, or give up and pot control.

crossSessionPatterns.trend: Not folding enough to opponent c-bets
crossSessionPatterns.action: When hero is OOP and faces a c-bet without equity (no pair, no draw), fold more. Currently over-defending vs c-bets — opponent range advantage is real.

crossSessionPatterns.trend: Consistently below 40% threshold — too many weak hands reaching showdown
crossSessionPatterns.action: Be more selective about which hands you call down. The issue: calling 3 streets with second pair, marginal top pair, or bluff-catchers with low equity.

crossSessionPatterns.pattern: UTG position bleed: -$174 (S1) → -$94 (S2) → $0 (S3, only 16 UTG hands at 0.50/1)
crossSessionPatterns.trend: UTG is consistently the biggest losing position across sessions
crossSessionPatterns.action: Apply verified UTG range strictly. Open only 23 core hands. ATo, KJo, QJo below mix threshold = fold from UTG.

spots.spot: KK postflop - wet board after 3-bet (Session 3 specific)
spots.scenario: KsKc 3-bet from BTN, board 9h8dJd (very wet). Villain leads or calls. Lost -$32.64 on 9-8-J-3-A board.
spots.recommendation: On wet 3-bet boards with an overpair: c-bet 75% pot to charge draws. When ace rivers and villain bets, fold KK — ace completed too many straights and top pairs. Pot control after flop c-bet if villain floats.
spots.keyLesson: Overpairs on wet boards: bet-fold flop, check-evaluate turn, fold river vs big bet on A/T/Q completions.

spots.spot: Steal discipline - default late position raise
spots.scenario: Sessions 2+3: steal rate 36-67% vs target 60-80%. Multiple missed BTN/CO/SB steal spots per session.
spots.recommendation: Install a rule: IF (folded to hero in CO/BTN/SB) AND (only BB left) THEN RAISE unless hand is truly unplayable. The profitability of stealing derives from fold equity + position + initiative — all three apply here.
spots.keyLesson: Default to raising in steal spots. The burden of proof is on FOLDING, not raising.

spots.scenario: 3 sessions: 0-5% 3-bet rate vs target 8-12%. Opponents stealing freely from BTN/SB.
spots.recommendation: Build 3-bet range in BB: TT+/AQs+/AKo as value, A5s-A4s/J2s/T3s as bluffs vs BTN. In SB vs BTN: 3-bet TT+/AQs+/KQs value, A5s-A3s/K7s bluffs. Stop flat-calling out of position with medium strength hands vs steals.
spots.keyLesson: 3-betting polarizes range, builds pots with strong hands, and denies equity to BTN steals. One 3-bet saves BB EV better than three folds.

spots.scenario: 0% fold-to-cbet for opponents across 22 hero c-bets over 3 sessions. Opponents are not folding.
spots.recommendation: 1) Check back more flops when you miss (especially high-card boards where villain's range is stronger). 2) When c-betting, use pot-size or overbet on dry boards only. 3) On wet boards without equity: check-fold or check-call once then give up. 4) Stop multi-barreling without improving.
spots.keyLesson: Against calling stations, c-betting is a tax, not a weapon. Save c-bets for when you have equity or a semi-bluff.
